@@ -560,6 +560,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.6","title":"Preflight JSONL authority before doctor rebuild repair","description":"## Purpose
Before `br doctor --repair` rebuilds SQLite from JSONL, prove the JSONL authority is safe enough to use. The existing 4.1-4.4 work classifies bad JSONL and validates after repair, but the repair branch should fail closed before moving DB artifacts if JSONL contains conflict markers, duplicate IDs, malformed records, or other non-authoritative state.

## Scope
- add an explicit preflight gate on the doctor JSONL rebuild path
- reuse existing JSONL conflict-marker and issue-record validators
- emit structured recovery/audit output that says rebuild was refused because JSONL was unsafe
- add regression coverage that the original DB family is not replaced when JSONL is unsafe

## Acceptance Criteria
- `br doctor --repair` refuses JSONL rebuild before DB replacement when JSONL is malformed or has conflict markers
- operator output names the JSONL authority failure and next safe action
- tests prove DB artifacts are preserved and no success verdict is emitted","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:21:15.360279513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:30:40.574506858Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:30:40.574139690Z","close_reason":"Added JSONL authority preflight before doctor rebuild repair; unsafe JSONL now refuses rebuild without moving DB artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","recovery","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.6","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:21:15.360279513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.7","title":"Validate relational state after JSONL rebuild recovery","description":"## Purpose
Extend post-recovery validation beyond issue row counts so a rebuild cannot silently drop relation data while still reporting success.

## Scope
- validate comments, labels, dependencies, events, dirty markers, export hashes, blocked cache, and child counters after JSONL rebuild recovery
- compare imported relational state against JSONL-derived expectations where available
- fail recovery with an actionable diagnostic if relation rows are missing, orphaned, or inconsistent after repair
- add tests with JSONL containing relation-rich issues and deliberately corrupted relation tables

## Acceptance Criteria
- recovery refuses success when non-issue user-visible state is lost or orphaned
- diagnostics identify the relation table and invariant that failed
- tests cover at least comments, labels, dependencies, and events or audit history","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:21:38.334483280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:21:38.334483280Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["invariants","recovery","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.7","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:21:38.334483280Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.8","title":"Stop repeated automatic recovery loops after failed verification","description":"## Purpose
Prevent repeated automatic recovery attempts from looping over the same damaged workspace after repair verification has already failed.

## Scope
- detect fresh recovery artifacts or audit evidence from a previous failed repair for the same DB family
- refuse another automatic rebuild unless the operator explicitly opts in after inspecting evidence
- emit clear guidance to preserve artifacts and escalate instead of cycling repair attempts
- add tests for second repair attempts after a failed post-repair verification

## Acceptance Criteria
- repeated repair attempts fail closed with a manual-intervention message
- no new quarantine layer is created unless the operator explicitly asks for another attempt
- audit output includes the prior failed phase and artifact location when available","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:21:49.434133103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:45:55.102757907Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:45:55.102415806Z","close_reason":"Added failed-recovery marker evidence and a repeated JSONL rebuild guard with explicit --allow-repeated-repair override","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","recovery","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.8","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:21:49.434133103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.9","title":"Verify recovery backups before database replacement","description":"## Purpose
Guarantee doctor repair never discards the active database unless the preserved recovery backup can be verified byte-for-byte.

## Scope
- add a backup verification step for DB-family artifacts created before JSONL rebuild or local quarantine
- fail closed if the copied artifact differs in size or digest from the source at copy time
- expose the verified backup path and digest in recovery audit details

## Acceptance Criteria
- backup verification is performed before database replacement continues
- verification failures abort repair before the source artifact is removed
- tests cover a successful backup verification round-trip and a failed verification refusal","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:47:44.002124593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:01:06.493467210Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:01:06.493178078Z","close_reason":"Verified recovery backup artifacts with size/hash fingerprints before database replacement and surfaced verification metadata in doctor recovery audits","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doctor","recovery","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.9","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-21T16:47:44.002124593Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","title":"Install release gates, observability, and operator guidance for reliability","description":"## Why
Reliability work is not complete when the code seems better locally. It is complete when regressions are hard to ship, anomalies are visible, and operators have a playbook for field failures.

## Scope
Turn the new invariants, fixture corpus, realism suites, and recovery paths into mandatory release discipline and operator guidance.

## Deliverables
- CI/release gates for real-world reliability suites
- structured observability around anomaly classification and recovery
- updated disaster-recovery and troubleshooting docs
- a non-hermetic smoke profile that exercises ambient-env and existing-workspace behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- reliability regressions become release blockers rather than post-release surprises
- operators can diagnose and escalate failures without reverse-engineering the code","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:54.326583926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:34.054171443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ops","release-gating","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:54.326583926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:18.751657304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:28.736789160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:37.692812501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":202,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists to turn architectural hardening into release discipline; otherwise the new realism work will remain optional and decay over time.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:57Z"},{"id":207,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track exists to prevent a repeat of the current situation where the suite is green but the field experience is red. The outcome has to be operational, not just architectural.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","title":"Make real-world reliability suites mandatory in CI and release verification","description":"## Purpose
Promote the real-world reliability suites from optional or ad hoc checks into mandatory gates for CI, release verification, and high-risk changes.

## Cover
- failure-corpus replay
- crash-injection suites
- long-lived-workspace stress
- doctor/recovery postcondition checks

## Acceptance Criteria
- shipping without these suites requires an explicit conscious override, not omission by default
- release verification documents the outcome of the reliability gates","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:57.611605676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:09:00.414820908Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:09:00.414550331Z","close_reason":"Added mandatory CI and release reliability gates with documented emergency override","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release-gating","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:53.826363038Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:03.126099578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:12.811292518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:22.300269735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:31.628692974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:57.611605676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":208,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"If the new realism suites do not gate merges and releases, they will slowly rot into optional diagnostics and we will be back where we started.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","title":"Add structured observability and audit records for anomaly and recovery paths","description":"## Purpose
Emit structured logs, audit records, and counters for anomaly classification, quarantine, recovery attempts, and post-recovery validation outcomes.

## Scope
- record what evidence was seen
- record which automatic path ran
- record whether the outcome was success, refusal, or degraded mode
- make the resulting data easy to correlate with operator reports and bug fixtures

## Acceptance Criteria
- field incidents can be mapped back to concrete anomaly and recovery states
- debugging no longer depends on sparse ad hoc stderr snippets","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:00.957711972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:32:00.972293878Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:32:00.972041656Z","close_reason":"Structured reliability audit records now expose doctor anomaly classification and repair outcomes with tracing and JSON fields.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","observability","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:41.051238985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:00.957711972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":209,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"A system that recovers or refuses without leaving a clear audit trail is still opaque. Observability is what turns one scary field report into reproducible engineering input.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:40Z"}]}
